guest.footerHint: You're using a one-click link from your confirmation email. Replies are recorded against this ticket.

home.title: Welcome back
home.subtitle: Track your support tickets and start new ones from here.
home.empty: You haven't filed any tickets yet.

signIn.title: Sign in to your support portal
signIn.subtitle: Use your password, or get a one-time link by email.

panel.title: Support, on your terms.
panel.subtitle: Submit a ticket, track every update, and reply right from your inbox.
panel.featureTrackDescription: See exactly where your request is — open, in progress, or resolved — in real time.
panel.featureReplyDescription: Continue any thread by replying to the email, or come back to the portal to attach files.
panel.featureFastDescription: We'll send a one-time link to your email. Click it and you're in — no password to remember.

signIn.guestSubmitPrefix: Just need to file a ticket?

errors.tooManyByEmail: Too many sign-in requests for this email. Try again later.
errors.tooManyByIp: Too many sign-in requests from your network. Try again later.
errors.expired: That sign-in link expired. Request a new one.
errors.accountNotFound: We don't have an account for that email. Use 'Create one' below to register first.
errors.unexpected: Something went wrong on our side. Try again in a moment.

signInSent.title: Check your email
signInSent.body: We sent a sign-in link to {email}. The link expires in 10 minutes.

signUpVerify.title: Confirm your email
signUpVerify.body: We sent a confirmation link to {email}. Click it to finish setting up your account.
signUpVerify.hint: Check your spam folder if you don't see it within a minute or two.

signUp.title: Create your support portal
signUp.subtitle: Set up your account in a minute.
signUp.phoneHint: We'll text you about ticket updates if you provide a number.
signUp.passwordHint: At least {min} characters. You'll use this to sign in (you can also use a magic-link email).
signUp.guestSubmitPrefix: Just need to file a ticket?

errors.invalidPhone: Phone must be in E.164 format (e.g. +14165550123).
errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 12 characters.

panel.title: Your support, in one place.
panel.subtitle: Create an account in seconds — no password, no hassle.
panel.featureTrackDescription: Every request you submit lives in your portal, with full history and status.
panel.featureFastDescription: We send you a magic link each time — nothing to remember, nothing to leak.
panel.featureUpdatesDescription: Email notifications when your ticket moves forward, with the option to reply right back.

list.title: My tickets
list.subtitle: Tickets you've submitted to our team.
list.empty: You haven't submitted any tickets yet.
list.emptyFiltered: No tickets match the current filters.

detail.closedNotice: This ticket is closed. Submit a new ticket if you need more help.

csat.title: Did this resolve your issue?
csat.subtitle: Confirming closes the ticket. If it didn't work, we'll reopen and get someone back on it.
csat.hint: Coordinators auto-close resolved tickets after 24 hours of no response.
csat.recapSatisfied: Marked as resolved. Thanks for confirming.
csat.recapUnsatisfied: Reopened for the team to take another look.
csat.recapThanks: We'll reach out if we need anything else.
csat.commentPlaceholder: What's still not working? The more detail, the faster we can help.
csat.commentTooLong: Comment is too long — please keep it under 2000 characters.

new.title: Submit a ticket
new.subtitle: Tell us what's wrong and we'll route it to the right team.
new.categoryOtherPlaceholder: What category does this fall under?
new.descriptionPlaceholder: What happened? What did you expect to happen? Anything you've tried?
new.subjectShort: Subject must be at least 3 characters.

errors.tooMany: You've reached today's ticket limit. Try again tomorrow.

reply.label: Reply
reply.closedNotice: You can't reply to a closed ticket.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Update your details and notification preferences.

avatar.hint: PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WEBP — up to {maxMb} MB.
avatar.errorUnsupportedType: Use a PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WEBP image.
avatar.errorTooLarge: Image is too large — max {maxMb} MB.

profile.phoneHint: Used for SMS notifications about your tickets. Leave empty to disable SMS.
profile.smsConfirmSent: Confirmation text sent to your new number.
profile.smsConfirmFailed: Saved, but we couldn't send a confirmation text to that number. Please check it can receive SMS.

password.setSubtitle: Optional — once set, you can sign in with a password instead of waiting for a magic link.
password.changeSubtitle: Re-enter your current password to set a new one.
password.revokeOthers: Sign me out everywhere else after changing
password.setSuccess: Password set. You can now sign in with it next time.
password.tooShort: Password must be at least {min} characters.
password.mismatch: New password and confirmation don't match.

notifications.title: Notification preferences
notifications.subtitle: Choose how you'd like to hear from us about your tickets.

events.ticket__agent_replied: When the team replies on my ticket
events.ticket__resolved: When my ticket is marked resolved

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.subtitle: Sign out from other devices you don't recognize.

errors.title: Something went wrong
errors.description: We hit an unexpected error loading your portal.

errors.validation: Please check the form for errors.
errors.permissionDenied: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.rateLimited: Too many requests. Try again in {minutes} minutes.
errors.unexpected: Something went wrong. Please try again.

boundary.title: Something went wrong
boundary.description: An unexpected error interrupted this page. The team has been notified.

admin.title: Something went wrong
admin.description: We hit an unexpected error loading this page.

notFoundPage.title: Page not found
notFoundPage.description: We couldn't find the page you're looking for.

reason.default: This action requires you to re-enter your password.
reason.settings: Changing app-wide settings requires a fresh password confirmation.
reason.superAdmin: Granting Super Admin access requires a fresh password confirmation.

queue.title: Tickets
queue.countMatching: {count, plural, =0 {No tickets} =1 {# ticket} other {# tickets}} matching "{query}"
queue.search: Search by ticket #, subject, or customer email
queue.empty: No tickets yet. They'll appear here as customers submit them.

detail.assigneeHelp: Choose which technician owns this ticket. Reassigning hands it off to a colleague; you can also add extra technicians who collaborate on it below.

actions.mergeDescription: Search for the ticket to merge {source} into (only tickets from the same organization are shown). All messages, attachments, and logged time from {source} move to it, both technicians are carried over, and {source} is closed as a duplicate.
actions.mergeSearchPlaceholder: Search by ticket name or ID…
actions.mergeNoResults: No matching tickets in this organization.
actions.mergedBannerHint: All conversation has moved to the linked ticket.

submit.title: Submit a ticket
submit.subtitle: Tell us what's wrong and a coordinator will route it to a technician.

fields.organizationHint: We use your email address to match your organization automatically.
fields.subject: Subject
fields.subjectAgentPlaceholder: Short summary as the customer described it
fields.categoryOtherPlaceholder: What category does this fall under?
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: What happened? What did you expect to happen? Anything you've tried?
fields.descriptionAgentPlaceholder: What did the customer report? Include any context they gave.

submit.captchaSkipped: Cloudflare Turnstile is not configured. In dev this is fine; in production it means NEXT_PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY isn't reaching the browser — submissions will fail captcha verification until it is set.
submit.attachmentsHint: Screenshots and logs help us diagnose faster.
submit.draftNeedsContact: Enter your name and email before attaching files.
submit.draftNeedsCaptcha: Complete the captcha before attaching files.
submit.chooseCategoryPriority: Please choose a category and priority.

validation.subjectShort: Subject must be at least 3 characters.
validation.descriptionShort: Description must be at least 20 characters.

errors.tooManyByIp: Too many submissions from your network. Try again in an hour.
errors.tooManyByEmail: Too many submissions from this email today. Try again tomorrow.
errors.captchaFailed: Captcha verification failed. Please refresh the page and try again.

submitSuccess.title: We've received your ticket
submitSuccess.ticketNumberLine: Your ticket number is {ticket}. A confirmation email is on its way — keep an eye on your inbox.
submitSuccess.trackingHelp: A coordinator will assign your ticket to a technician shortly. To add details or a screenshot, just reply to the confirmation email.

createOnBehalf.title: Create ticket on behalf
createOnBehalf.subtitle: Use this when a customer reports an issue by phone or in person. The customer will receive the standard confirmation email.

resolveModal.title: Resolve ticket
resolveModal.description: Describe what fixed the issue. The note is stored on the ticket and sent to the customer with their CSAT request.
resolveModal.minLengthError: Resolution note must be at least 10 characters.
resolveModal.skipCheckboxHint: For duplicates, customer cancellations, or similar — the reason is logged in the audit trail.
resolveModal.skipDescription: No resolution note will be sent to the customer. Briefly explain why this ticket can be resolved without one.
resolveModal.skipPlaceholder: Reason for skipping (e.g., duplicate of AX-0034, customer cancelled)…
resolveModal.skipReasonMinError: Skip reason must be at least 10 characters.

billable.title: Billable
billable.help: How this ticket's work is billed. Billable / per hour: charged by the hours logged. Monthly plan: hours are deducted from the organization's included balance. Project basis: billed as part of a project. Rework: redo of earlier work. No: not billable.

orgControl.title: Organization
orgControl.help: Set or change the organization this ticket belongs to — or mark it as having none. Same as the triage queue, available here so a dismissed or mis-matched ticket isn't stuck.

workLog.title: Work log
workLog.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. Replaced failed RAM module and ran diagnostics
workLog.timeRequired: Enter how long the work took.

escalateModal.escalateDescription: The ticket stays assigned to you. The role you escalate to is notified to review.
escalateModal.notePlaceholder: Anything else the escalation target should know? (max 1000 characters)

reply.placeholder: Write a reply to the customer…
reply.internalPlaceholder: Write an internal note (not sent to the customer)…
reply.footerHint: Reply is emailed to the customer.
reply.internalFooterHint: Internal note — only visible to the team.

filters.active: {count, plural, =0 {No filters} =1 {1 filter active} other {# filters active}}

rowActions.assignDescription: Pick a technician, coordinator, or director to handle this ticket.
rowActions.editDescription: Change the assignee. For status changes, escalation, replies, and other edits, open the full ticket.
rowActions.deleteDescription: Soft-deletes the ticket and hides it from all queues. The audit trail and message history are preserved.
rowActions.deleteConfirmMismatch: Type the exact ticket number to confirm.
rowActions.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

csat.satisfiedBody: Thanks for confirming — we've closed the ticket. If anything comes back, just open a new one and we'll pick it up.
csat.unsatisfiedBody: We've reopened the ticket and a technician will follow up. Watch your inbox for the next update.
csat.invalidBody: The CSAT link in your email looks expired or invalid. If you still need to give feedback, reply to the resolution email and we'll capture it manually.
csat.alreadyResponded: We already had your answer on file — nothing else changed.

attachments.label: Attachments
attachments.prepareFailed: Couldn't start the upload. Complete the form above first.
attachments.tooManyFiles: You can attach up to {max} files per message.

list.title: Users
list.subtitle: Create, edit, deactivate, and reset accounts.
list.tabHintInternal: Staff who can be assigned tickets — Super Admin, IT Director, Coordinator, Technician.
list.tabHintExternal: Customers who file tickets via the portal or email.

rowActions.editDescription: Update name and role assignments. Sessions, password reset, and deactivation cascade live on the full profile.
rowActions.deactivateDescription: Choosing what happens to descendants (move-up vs reassign) is required. We'll take you to the full profile to pick — the user stays active until you confirm there.
rowActions.reactivateDescription: The user can sign in again immediately. You can re-deactivate later if needed.

create.title: Create user
create.subtitle: The user will receive a welcome email at the address you enter.
create.welcomeEmailHint: The user receives a welcome email with a link to set their own password — no password to share.

edit.title: Edit user
edit.subtitle: Update profile, language, and role assignments.

fields.name: Full name
fields.phoneHint: E.164 format. Receives SMS alerts (assignment, SLA breach, escalation).

deactivate.title: Deactivate user
deactivate.description: What should happen to users they created?
deactivate.optionMoveUpNoParent: Move descendants up (no parent — they'll have no creator)
deactivate.optionCascade: Cascade — also deactivate {count, plural, =1 {# descendant} other {# descendants}}
deactivate.directChildren: {count, plural, =0 {No direct reports} =1 {# direct report} other {# direct reports}}
deactivate.totalDescendants: {count, plural, =0 {No descendants} =1 {# total descendant} other {# total descendants}}

list.title: Procurement
list.subtitle: Hardware and software requests against tickets.

section.title: Procurement requests
section.empty: No procurement requests for this ticket.

form.title: New procurement request
form.justificationHint: Why is this needed? Minimum 10 characters.

create.title: Add organization
create.subtitle: Register a client company so its tickets carry its code and its contract is tracked.

edit.title: Edit {name}
edit.subtitle: Update the organization's details and contract balance.
edit.readOnly: You don't have permission to edit this organization.
edit.planNegative: Over plan by {hours}. The accountants have been notified.

trusted.cardSubtitle: People outside this organization's domains whose ticket replies you've chosen to trust. Their replies post automatically; remove someone to moderate them again.
trusted.empty: No trusted contacts. Use “Approve & trust sender” in Moderation to add one.

addHours.label: Add hours to this plan
addHours.hint: Adds to the hours-remaining balance for this month (e.g. a top-up when the plan runs short). This is the only manual way to change the balance.
addHours.invalid: Enter a positive number of hours.

usage.noneThisMonth: No work logged this month yet.
usage.noWork: No work has been logged for this organization yet.

fields.name: Organization name
fields.abbreviationHint: 2–5 letters or digits used as the ticket-number prefix (e.g. KI → KI-20260522-001). Auto-generated from the name; edit it or generate a new one.
fields.codeTaken: That code is already in use — pick or generate a different one.
fields.codeInvalid: Code must be 2–5 letters or digits.
fields.emailDomainsHint: One per line. Guests whose email matches a domain here are linked to this organization automatically (e.g. kingsmill.com).
fields.monthlyHoursBalanceHint: Defaults to the included hours. Monthly-plan work deducts from this balance.
fields.monthlyHoursBalanceReadonlyHint: Read-only. Changes only through logged work, the monthly reset, and the “Add hours” action.

rowActions.deleteDescription: This permanently removes the organization. It is refused if any tickets or users still reference it — deactivate it instead.

list.title: Roles
list.subtitle: Custom roles and per-module permission grants.

rowActions.editDescription: Update name and description. Permission grants are edited on the full role page.
rowActions.deleteDescription: Removes the role and its permission grants. Anyone holding the role must be reassigned first.

create.title: Create role
create.subtitle: Pick a name and the permissions this role should grant.

edit.title: Edit role
edit.subtitle: Update the role's name, description, and permissions.
edit.deleteConfirm: Delete this role? This can't be undone.
edit.systemEditableBadge: System role — you're the Super Admin, so its permissions are editable.

moduleDescription.tickets: Submit, assign, and resolve customer support requests.
moduleDescription.procurement: Hardware and software purchase requests tied to tickets.
moduleDescription.reports: Ticket health, SLA compliance, and procurement spend.
moduleDescription.roles: Custom roles and per-action permission grants.
moduleDescription.settings: SLA targets, business hours, email, and security knobs.
moduleDescription.audit: Append-only record of every privileged action.

matrix.lockedTooltip: You don't hold this permission yourself, so you can't grant it. Ask the Super Admin.
matrix.lockedHidden: {count, plural, =1 {1 permission hidden — Locked} other {# permissions hidden — Locked}}

description.tickets__view: Read the ticket queue and ticket detail pages.
description.tickets__create: File a new ticket on behalf of a customer.
description.tickets__update: Edit ticket fields like priority and category.
description.tickets__assign: Pick a technician to handle a ticket.
description.tickets__reply: Send replies to the customer (visible in their portal).
description.tickets__internal_note: Add notes only the team can see.
description.tickets__resolve: Mark a ticket resolved (requires a resolution note).
description.tickets__resolve_skip_note: Bypass the resolution-note requirement in edge cases (audited).
description.tickets__reopen: Reopen a resolved or closed ticket.
description.tickets__escalate: Flag a ticket as needing senior attention.
description.worklog__view_all: See work-log entries across all technicians, not just your own.
description.organizations__view: View client organizations and their details.
description.organizations__update: Edit organization details, domains, and plans.
description.procurement__create: File a hardware or software purchase request.
description.procurement__manage: Move requests through the order stages.
description.users__view: Read the user list and user profiles.
description.users__create: Create new staff or customer accounts.
description.users__update: Edit user names, language, and role assignments.
description.users__reset_password: Send a password reset link to a user.
description.users__unlock: Clear an account-lockout from too many failed sign-ins.
description.roles__view: Read the role list and role configurations.
description.roles__update: Edit role names, descriptions, and permission grants.
description.roles__delete: Remove a role (only when no users hold it).

page.title: System settings
page.subtitle: Live values used across the app. Changes take effect immediately.

billing.title: Accountant notifications
billing.subtitle: Who receives billing alerts — when an organization goes over its support plan, and when a ticket is resolved. These are your accountants, not the customers'.
billing.emailsHint: Receive over-plan and ticket-billing emails. Add one address at a time.
billing.phonesHint: Receive the over-plan SMS alert. Use E.164 format, e.g. +15551234567.
billing.superadminHint: Send the same accountant notifications to every active Super Admin's own email and phone.

accountantPhones.empty: No accountant phone numbers configured yet.

moderation.title: Inbound reply moderation
moderation.description: Controls what happens when someone who isn't the ticket's customer, a participant, or part of its organization replies to a ticket email.
moderation.toggleHint: On (recommended): such replies are held in the Moderation queue for review. Off: every reply posts straight to the conversation thread.

branding.title: Brand identity
branding.subtitle: Wordmark, accent color, and background gradient used on sign-in and submit pages.
branding.brandNameHint: Main part of the wordmark, rendered in foreground color.
branding.brandAccentHint: Optional suffix rendered in the accent color (e.g. "360").

businessHours.title: Business hours
businessHours.subtitle: SLA timers honor these for priorities flagged respect_business_hours.
businessHours.timezoneHint: Used for business hours, SLA timers, and all dates & times shown across the app.

sla.title: SLA targets
sla.subtitle: Response and resolution targets per priority, in minutes.

holidays.title: Holidays
holidays.subtitle: Days excluded from SLA business-hours computation.

domains.title: Internal email domains
domains.subtitle: Submitter emails on these domains are auto-tagged stream=internal.

procurement.title: Procurement
procurement.thresholdHint: 0 disables the second step — every request is single-step Coordinator approval.

responseWindow.title: Customer response window
responseWindow.label: Hours before resolved tickets auto-close without CSAT response

unassignedAlert.title: Unassigned-ticket alerts
unassignedAlert.subtitle: Email admins when a ticket sits with no technician assigned.
unassignedAlert.enabledHint: When on, Coordinators, IT Directors and Super Admins are emailed about tickets left without an owner.
unassignedAlert.thresholdHint: How long a ticket may sit unassigned before the first alert. Default 120 (2 hours).
unassignedAlert.repeatHint: Re-send the alert this often until the ticket is assigned. Set 0 to alert only once.

emails.title: Email configuration
emails.inboundDomainHint: Used for ticket+AX-XXXX@your-domain.com threading. Read-only after first set.
emails.allowlistDescription: When on, only emails from registered customer accounts are accepted; others get an auto-reply.

fileUpload.title: File uploads
fileUpload.maxSizeHint: Per-file size limit in megabytes. Default 10 MB. Hard-capped at 10 MB by the database constraint.
fileUpload.maxFilesHint: Per reply or new ticket. Default 5, range 1–20.
fileUpload.empty: No allowed MIME types — uploads will be rejected.

virusScan.title: Virus scanning
virusScan.description: When on, every confirmed upload is queued for scanning before being marked clean. Infected files are quarantined and removed from storage.
virusScan.providerHint: EICAR is for end-to-end testing only — it just looks for the EICAR test signature. Use clamav-rest in production.
virusScan.endpointHint: HTTPS URL of the clamav-rest service. Required when the provider is ClamAV (REST).

page.title: Audit log
page.subtitle: Append-only record of every privileged action.

moderation.title: Inbound moderation
moderation.subtitle: Email replies from senders outside the ticket's organization are held here. Approve & post the reply, optionally trusting the sender so their future replies post automatically, or reject it.
moderation.empty: Nothing to moderate — no held replies.
moderation.heldNote: Held: this sender isn't recognized as the ticket's customer, a participant, or part of its organization.
moderation.trustHint: “Trust” posts this reply and lets future replies from this person post automatically for the ticket's organization (revoke anytime on the organization page). “Approve once” posts only this reply — they'll be moderated again next time.

orgTriage.title: Organization triage
orgTriage.subtitle: Guest tickets whose email domain didn't match a registered organization. Link each to the right one by their email, create a new organization, or mark it as having none.
orgTriage.empty: Nothing to triage — every ticket's organization is confirmed.
orgTriage.banner: {count, plural, =1 {# ticket needs an organization} other {# tickets need an organization}}.

timesheet.title: Work Log
timesheet.subtitleAll: Time logged across all tickets and technicians.
timesheet.subtitleOwn: Time you've logged across your tickets.
timesheet.empty: No work logged for these filters yet.

addModal.title: Log time
addModal.description: Record time you spent working on a ticket.
addModal.ticketRequired: Select a ticket to log time against.
addModal.noTickets: You have no open tickets to log time against. Open a ticket you're assigned to and log work from there.

rowActions.editDescription: Update the work description, time spent, or service type.
rowActions.deleteDescription: This removes the logged time from {ticket}. This can't be undone.

page.title: My profile
page.subtitle: Account info, security, sessions, and notification preferences.

account.title: Account
account.name: Name
account.phoneHint: E.164 format. Receives SMS alerts (assignment, SLA breach, escalation).
account.smsConfirmSent: Confirmation text sent to your new number.
account.smsConfirmFailed: Saved, but we couldn't send a confirmation text to that number. Please check it can receive SMS.

password.title: Password
password.revokeOthers: Sign me out of all other sessions

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.subtitle: Sessions currently signed in to your account.
sessions.revokeOthers: Sign out of all other sessions

preferences.title: Notification preferences
preferences.subtitle: Email and SMS for each event type. In-app notifications always show in the bell.

bell.unreadAriaLive: {count, plural, =1 {You have 1 unread notification} other {You have # unread notifications}}

message_held.title: Reply held for review on {ticketNumber}
message_held.body: {sender} replied from outside the organization. Review it in moderation.

resolved.title: Ticket {ticketNumber} resolved
resolved.body: Your ticket has been resolved. Confirm in the portal to close it.

agent_replied.title: New reply on {ticketNumber}
agent_replied.body: The team replied to your ticket.

warning_80.title: {ticketNumber} — SLA at 80%
warning_80.body: {kind} target close to breach. Update or escalate.

quarantined.title: Attachment quarantined on {ticketNumber}
quarantined.body: {fileName} matched {signature} and was removed from storage.

ticketAssignedCustomer.body: {ticketNumber}: A technician is now assigned to your ticket. View: {ticketUrl}

agentReplied.body: {ticketNumber}: New reply on your ticket. View: {ticketUrl}

ticketResolved.body: {ticketNumber}: Your ticket has been resolved. Confirm or reopen: {ticketUrl}

ticketReopened.body: {ticketNumber}: Your ticket has been reopened. View: {ticketUrl}

ticketClosed.body: {ticketNumber}: Your ticket is now closed. View: {ticketUrl}

phoneChanged.body: Axiom360: this number is now set for your account alerts. If you didn't do this, contact your administrator.

accountantNegativeBalance.body: {orgName} is {overHours} over its monthly support plan. Over-plan hours are billable.

shell.topbarSearchHintEmpty: Type a ticket number, name, or email to search.

login.title: Staff sign-in
login.subtitle: Use your work email to access the admin console.
login.passwordSetSuccess: Password set. Sign in below to continue.

panel.title: Run support that scales with your team.
panel.subtitle: Triage, route, and resolve — with audit trails, SLAs, and procurement built in.
panel.featureQueueDescription: Email, portal, and in-app tickets land in the same pipeline with deduping baked in.
panel.featureSlaDescription: Business-hours-aware timers, breach alerts, and visible deadlines on every ticket.
panel.featureGovernanceDescription: Role-aware permissions and an append-only audit log of every privileged action.

setup.title: Set your password
setup.subtitle: Pick a password to finish setting up your account.
setup.errorTooShort: Password must be at least 12 characters.
setup.missingTokenBody: This page expects a setup token in the URL. Open the link from your welcome email.

landing.cardTicketsDescription: Triage the queue, assign work, reply to customers.
landing.cardProcurementDescription: Hardware and software requests against tickets.
landing.cardUsersDescription: Create staff, edit profiles, deactivate accounts.
landing.cardRolesDescription: Custom roles and per-module permission grants.
landing.cardHierarchyDescription: See who created whom — the org tree.
landing.cardReportsDescription: Ticket health, SLA compliance, procurement spend.
landing.cardSettingsDescription: SLA targets, business hours, email, security.
landing.cardAuditDescription: Append-only record of every privileged action.

hierarchy.title: Hierarchy
hierarchy.subtitle: Who created whom. The tree backs the impersonation + descendant-cascade rules.

stubs.procurementDescription: Hardware and software purchase requests with two-step approval.
stubs.usersDescription: Create, deactivate, reset password, and impersonate users.
stubs.rolesDescription: Custom role creation with the per-module permissions matrix.
stubs.hierarchyDescription: User-creation tree showing who created whom.
stubs.reportsDescription: Ticket health metrics and procurement spend dashboards.
stubs.auditDescription: Filterable view of every privileged action.
stubs.settingsDescription: SLA targets, business hours, internal email domains, and other configurables.
stubs.profileDescription: Account info, password, sessions, and notification preferences.

shared.footerLine2: This is an automated message. Reply to this email to add to your ticket.

ticketCreated.title: We've received your ticket
ticketCreated.body: Thanks for reaching out. Your ticket {ticketNumber} — “{subject}” — has been received and a coordinator will assign it to a technician shortly.
ticketCreated.replyHint: You'll hear from us as soon as it's assigned. To add details or a screenshot, just reply to this email.
ticketCreated.fallback: If the link above doesn't work, copy and paste this URL into your browser:
ticketCreated.subject: [{ticketNumber}] We've received your ticket: {subject}

ticketCreatedStaff.title: New ticket received
ticketCreatedStaff.body: {customerName} submitted a new ticket {ticketNumber}. Triage it and assign a technician.
ticketCreatedStaff.trailerLine: Assign or triage this ticket from the admin console.
ticketCreatedStaff.subject: [{ticketNumber}] New ticket: {subject}

slaBreachedStaff.title: SLA breached
slaBreachedStaff.body: Ticket {ticketNumber} has missed its {kind} SLA target and needs immediate attention.
slaBreachedStaff.trailerLine: Reassign, escalate, or respond from the admin console.
slaBreachedStaff.subject: [{ticketNumber}] SLA BREACHED — action needed

ticketUnassignedStaff.title: Ticket still unassigned
ticketUnassignedStaff.body: Ticket {ticketNumber} has been waiting with no technician assigned. Please assign someone.
ticketUnassignedStaff.trailerLine: Assign a technician from the admin console.
ticketUnassignedStaff.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Still unassigned — needs an owner

ticketClosedStaff.title: Ticket closed
ticketClosedStaff.reason: {reason, select, csat {Closed after the customer confirmed the resolution.} auto {Auto-closed after 24 hours with no customer response.} other {Closed.}}
ticketClosedStaff.trailerLine: View the closed ticket in the admin console.
ticketClosedStaff.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Ticket closed: {subject}

ticketAssigned.preview: Your ticket {ticketNumber} is now being worked on
ticketAssigned.title: Your ticket is now being worked on
ticketAssigned.body: Good news — {technicianName} has picked up your ticket {ticketNumber} and is looking into “{subject}”.
ticketAssigned.footer: You'll get an update as soon as they have something to share. You can also reply to this email at any time to add details.
ticketAssigned.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Your ticket has been assigned: {subject}

ticketReply.title: {agentName} replied to your ticket
ticketReply.intro: We've sent you an update on “{subject}”:
ticketReply.footer: You can reply to this email to keep the conversation going, or open the ticket in your browser.
ticketReply.trailerLine: Reply to this email or click the link above to respond. Mention ticket {ticketNumber} if your mail client doesn't keep the thread.
ticketReply.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Re: {subject}

ticketResolved.title: Your ticket has been resolved
ticketResolved.body: {agentName} has resolved your ticket {ticketNumber} — “{subject}”. Here's what they did:
ticketResolved.feedbackPrompt: Did this fix the issue? Your feedback closes the loop. If we don't hear back within 24 hours, the ticket will close automatically.
ticketResolved.viewLine: You can also <link>view the ticket</link> or reply to this email if you need to add more details.
ticketResolved.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Resolved — was this fixed? {subject}

ticketClosed.title: Your ticket is closed
ticketClosed.bodyCsat: Thanks for confirming — ticket {ticketNumber} “{subject}” is now closed.
ticketClosed.bodyAuto: We hadn't heard back on ticket {ticketNumber} “{subject}”, so we've closed it for you. If the issue comes back, just open a new ticket and we'll pick it up.
ticketClosed.newTicketPrompt: Need help with something else? Open a fresh ticket any time.
ticketClosed.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Ticket closed: {subject}

ticketReopened.title: Your ticket has been reopened
ticketReopened.bodyCsat: Sorry our last fix didn't do it. Ticket {ticketNumber} “{subject}” is reopened and we're back on it. A technician will follow up shortly.
ticketReopened.bodyAgent: Ticket {ticketNumber} “{subject}” has been reopened by our team and assigned for follow-up.
ticketReopened.footer: If there's any extra detail that would help us, just reply to this email and we'll add it to the ticket.
ticketReopened.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Ticket reopened: {subject}

escalationAlert.title: A ticket has been escalated
escalationAlert.guidance: The ticket is still assigned to {technicianName}. Please review and either take it over, advise, or de-escalate when ready.
escalationAlert.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Ticket escalated: {subject}

ticketReassigned.preview: Ticket {ticketNumber} was reassigned to another technician
ticketReassigned.title: A ticket was reassigned
ticketReassigned.intro: A ticket has been reassigned from one technician to another.
ticketReassigned.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Ticket reassigned: {subject}

csatUnsatisfiedStaff.preview: Customer reopened ticket {ticketNumber} — still not resolved
csatUnsatisfiedStaff.title: Customer says the ticket is still not resolved
csatUnsatisfiedStaff.body: Ticket {ticketNumber} “{subject}” was marked resolved, but the customer pushed back. The ticket has been reopened.
csatUnsatisfiedStaff.guidance: Please review the latest customer comment on the ticket and follow up.
csatUnsatisfiedStaff.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Customer reopened — still not resolved

customerRepliedStaff.title: New customer reply
customerRepliedStaff.body: {customerName} added a new message to ticket {ticketNumber}.
customerRepliedStaff.trailerLine: Reply from the ticket in the admin console to respond to {ticketNumber}.
customerRepliedStaff.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Customer replied

inboundBounce.preview: We couldn't match your email to a ticket
inboundBounce.title: We couldn't match your email to a ticket
inboundBounce.body: Thanks for getting in touch. Your message didn't reference an existing ticket, so we weren't sure where to file it. To make sure we see it, please open a new ticket through our portal.
inboundBounce.cta: It only takes a minute — and we'll respond from there.
inboundBounce.subject: We couldn't match your email to a ticket

inboundClosedTicket.preview: Ticket {ticketNumber} is closed — please open a new one
inboundClosedTicket.title: That ticket is already closed
inboundClosedTicket.body: Your reply came in on ticket {ticketNumber}, which is already closed. We don't reopen tickets from email replies, so the conversation isn't going to reach the team this way.
inboundClosedTicket.cta: If the issue is back or you have something new, please open a fresh ticket and we'll pick it up there.
inboundClosedTicket.subject: [{ticketNumber}] That ticket is closed — please open a new one

procurementApproved.preview: Your procurement request has been approved
procurementApproved.title: Procurement request approved
procurementApproved.body: Your request for {quantity} × {itemName} has been approved. We'll mark it purchased once the order goes out.
procurementApproved.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Procurement approved: {itemName}

procurementRejected.preview: Your procurement request has been rejected
procurementRejected.title: Procurement request rejected
procurementRejected.body: Your request for {itemName} has been rejected. The reviewer left this note:
procurementRejected.nextSteps: If you'd like to try again, submit a fresh request from the ticket.
procurementRejected.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Procurement rejected: {itemName}

procurementDelivered.preview: Your procurement order has been delivered
procurementDelivered.title: Procurement order delivered
procurementDelivered.body: {quantity} × {itemName} has been delivered. The ticket is updated.
procurementDelivered.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Delivered: {itemName}

attachmentQuarantined.title: An attachment was quarantined
attachmentQuarantined.body: We removed {fileName} from “{subject}” because the virus scanner matched it against {signature}.
attachmentQuarantined.note: The file has been deleted from storage. If you need to reshare it, please verify the file is clean and upload again.
attachmentQuarantined.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Attachment quarantined: {fileName}

attachmentRemovedCustomer.preview: An attachment on {ticketNumber} couldn't be added
attachmentRemovedCustomer.title: We couldn't add your attachment
attachmentRemovedCustomer.body: The file {fileName} you added to ticket {ticketNumber} didn't pass our security scan, so we weren't able to attach it.
attachmentRemovedCustomer.guidance: Please check the file on your device and, if you're confident it's safe, try uploading it again — or reply to let us know what it contained.
attachmentRemovedCustomer.subject: [{ticketNumber}] We couldn't add your attachment: {fileName}

accountLockout.preview: Your account has been temporarily locked
accountLockout.title: Your account has been temporarily locked
accountLockout.body: We saw {attempts} failed sign-in attempts on your account in a short window, so we've temporarily locked it as a precaution. The lock lifts automatically in {minutes} minutes.
accountLockout.ifYou: If this was you and you've forgotten your password, use the password reset flow once the lock lifts. If it wasn't you, please change your password right after sign-in and consider enabling two-factor authentication.
accountLockout.support: If you need to get back in sooner, contact your administrator — they can unlock the account.
accountLockout.subject: Your Axiom360 account has been temporarily locked

customerMagicLink.preview: Sign in to your Axiom360 support portal
customerMagicLink.title: Sign in to your support portal
customerMagicLink.body: Click the button below to sign in. No password needed.
customerMagicLink.expiry: This link expires in 10 minutes and can only be used once.
customerMagicLink.ifNotYou: If you didn't request this email, you can safely ignore it.
customerMagicLink.subject: Your Axiom360 sign-in link

customerEmailVerification.preview: Confirm your email to finish creating your account
customerEmailVerification.title: Confirm your email
customerEmailVerification.body: Thanks for signing up. To finish setting up your support portal account, please confirm this is your email address.
customerEmailVerification.ifNotYou: If you didn't sign up for Axiom360 support, you can safely ignore this email.
customerEmailVerification.subject: Confirm your Axiom360 support email

staffSetupInvite.previewSet: Set your Axiom360 password to get started
staffSetupInvite.bodySet: Hi {name}, your Axiom360 account has been created. Click below to set your password and sign in.
staffSetupInvite.bodyReset: Hi {name}, click below to set a new password for your Axiom360 account.
staffSetupInvite.expiry: This link is valid for 24 hours. After that, ask an admin to send a new one.
staffSetupInvite.ifNotYou: If you weren't expecting this email, you can safely ignore it.
staffSetupInvite.subject: Set your Axiom360 password

customerWelcome.preview: Welcome to your Axiom360 support portal
customerWelcome.title: Welcome to your support portal
customerWelcome.claimedBody: {count, plural, =1 {We found 1 existing ticket linked to your email and added it to your portal.} other {We found # existing tickets linked to your email and added them to your portal.}}
customerWelcome.noClaimedBody: Your portal is ready. You can submit and track tickets from here.
customerWelcome.subject: Welcome to your Axiom360 support portal

accountantNegativeBalance.preview: {orgName} has gone over its monthly support hours
accountantNegativeBalance.title: Monthly plan over balance
accountantNegativeBalance.body: {orgName} has used all of its included support hours and is now {overHours} over its monthly plan.
accountantNegativeBalance.guidance: Over-plan hours are billable. Please review and invoice the overage as appropriate.
accountantNegativeBalance.subject: Over plan: {orgName} is out of support hours

accountantTicketBilling.title: Ticket resolved — billing
accountantTicketBilling.intro: Ticket {ticketNumber} for {orgName} has been resolved. Here is the billing summary:
accountantTicketBilling.subject: [{ticketNumber}] Resolved — billing summary

status.billed: Covered by the monthly support plan — no invoice needed.
status.pending: An amount is pending — please invoice this work{overHours, select, none {} other { ({overHours} over plan)}}.
status.none: Nothing to bill for this ticket.
status.review: Needs review — this ticket isn't categorized for billing yet.
